pick.rationale: Despite the significant loss of star freshman Caleb Wilson, North Carolina maintains a superior analytical profile with KenPom rank 29 vs VCU's 46, and the Tar Heels have gone 5-3 without Wilson, showing resilience. VCU is a dangerous 11-seed with tournament experience and a fast-paced style, but UNC's home-court advantage in Greenville, SC, and their overall talent depth give them a slight edge in this closely contested 6/11 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: North Carolina vs VCU Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluating the impact of Caleb Wilson's injury on North Carolina against a surging VCU team.
reasoningStep.evidence: North Carolina's KenPom rank is 29 (Torvik 27) vs VCU's 46 (Torvik 51), with UNC having better offensive and defensive efficiency rankings. UNC is 5-3 without Wilson, indicating they can still compete at a high level. VCU has a 26-7 record and won the A-10 tournament, but their defensive efficiency (rank 82) is weaker than UNC's (rank 34). The betting line of UNC -2.5 suggests a tight game, but UNC's proximity to Greenville provides a pseudo-home-court advantage.

result.text: UNC opens NCAA Tournament without Caleb Wilson vs. surging VCU :: WRAL.com UNC opens NCAA Tournament without Caleb Wilson vs. surging VCU No. 6 seed North Carolina begins its NCAA Tournament run Thursday against No. 11 seed VCU while adjusting to life without star freshman Caleb Wilson. The Tar Heels have gone 5-3 since Wilson’s season-ending thumb injury. Posted 3/17/2026, 12:51:55 AM Updated 3/17/2026, 4:37:53 PM Related UNC sports UNC basketball Hubert Davis college basketball NCAA Tournament March Madness Selection Sunday Hubert Davis on VCU: We'll be ready for the challenge By Mark Bergin, WRAL senior multiplatform producer North Carolina will have somewhat of a homecourt advantage in the opening weekend of the 2026 NCAA Tournament if the Tar Heels can manage without star freshman Caleb Wilson. No. 6 seed UNC (24-8) plays No. 11 seed VCU (27-7) at 6:50 p.m. ET Thursday at Bon Secours Wellness Arena in Greenville, South Carolina. The South Region game airs on TNT. Other WRAL Top Stories Wilson, an All-ACC first-team selection, is out for the season after breaking his right thumb. Wilson injured the thumb while working back from a left hand fracture. Wilson's last game was on Feb. 20. UNC is 5-3 in eight games without Wilson. "Our team will move forward," UNC coach Hubert Davis said of Wilson's injury on March 7 after the regular-season finale against Duke. Davis added, "Obviously, there's tremendous sadness for him. I've talked about how special ... obviously he's a special player, but just how remarkable of a kid and a teammate he is and the passion that he has for his teammates, for North Carolina for being on the floor, for playing in games like this. It was a dream for him to play in the ACC [Tournament] and the NCAA Tournament, and my heart is broken that he won't be able to do that." The UNC-VCU winner will play the winner of the Illinois-Penn game on Saturday in Greenville, South Carolina. The other teams in the South Region include No. 1 seed Florida, No. 2 seed Houston, No. 4 seed Nebraska, No. 5 seed Vanderbilt, No. 7 seed Saint Mary’s, No. 8 seed Clemson, No. 9 seed Iowa, No. 10 seed Texas A&M, No. 12 seed McNeese, No. 13 seed Troy, No. 15 seed, Idaho, No. 16 seed Lehigh and No. 16 seed Prairie View A&M. UNC enters the NCAA Tournament on a two-game losing streak. Thursday marks the Tar Heels’ 55th appearance in the NCAA Tournament, the second most all-time. It is the fourth NCAA Tournament appearance in five seasons under head coach Hubert Davis, who has led the Tar Heels to an 8-3 record in the NCAA Tournament. The Tar Heels fell to Duke in the regular-season finale and lost 80-79 to Clemson in the ACC quarterfinals . Meanwhile, the Rams have won 16 of their last 17 games. It includes a three-game sweep of Duquesne, Saint Joseph's and Dayton to win the A-10 title this past weekend in Pittsburgh. result.text: UNC vs. VCU: What to know about the Rams' key players Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports Three VCU players UNC should be worried about North Carolina draws a dangerous No. 11 seed VCU in the NCAA Tournament. Here’s what to know about the Rams’ key players. Grant Chachere Tar Heels Wire North Carolina opens NCAA Tournament play in the South Region on Thursday, and unlike past years when it came in as a No. 6 seed or lower, the Tar Heels draw a dangerous first-round matchup with No. 11 seed VCU . The Rams have reached the NCAA Tournament 15 times since 2004. In those 15 appearances, they have advanced out of the first round five times, a stretch that includes a win over No. 6 seed Duke in 2007 and their memorable Cinderella run to the Final Four in 2011. With an expectation of not just making the field but winning games in March, VCU represents a far tougher opening test than many of the Tar Heels’ recent first-round opponents. VCU enters the tournament at 27-7 overall and went 15-3 in Atlantic 10 play, sharing the regular-season title with Dayton. The Rams captured the A-10 tournament championship with wins over Duquesne in the quarterfinals, Saint Joseph’s in the semifinals and Dayton in the title game. The Rams are 2-5 against Quad 1 opponents, with victories over South Florida and a road win at Dayton, and losses to NC State, Vanderbilt, Utah State and Saint Louis twice. Here are three players from the Rams to keep an eye on. Guard Terrence Hill (6-3, 180) Hill, one of two Rams named first-team All-Atlantic 10, is a combo guard who can score at all three levels and is an effective slasher, adept at creating drive-and-kick opportunities. Despite coming off the bench, Hill leads VCU in scoring at 14.4 points per game and in assists at 2.8 per game, earning Atlantic 10 sixth man of the year honors. Forward/Center Lazar Djokovic (6-11, 235) Djokovic is the Rams’ other first-team All-Atlantic 10 pick and a crucial presence in the frontcourt. At 6-foot-11, he is an athletic, skilled big who thrives in pick-and-roll action and can stretch defenses with his shooting. Djokovic is hitting 35.6% from 3-point range and is averaging 13.5 points and 5.4 rebounds while shooting 51.4% from the field. Guard Nyk Lewis (6-1, 195) Lewis was named the Atlantic 10 Freshman of the Year after averaging 8.7 points and 4.5 rebounds. A former top-100 recruit, he chose VCU over several power-conference programs. He also was a high school teammate of North Carolina point guard Derek Dixon at Gonzaga College High School in Washington, D.C. Follow us @TarHeelsWire on X and like our page on Facebook to follow ongoing coverage of North Carolina Tar Heels news, notes and opinions. result.text: UNC opens NCAA Tournament without Caleb Wilson vs. surging VCU :: WRAL.com UNC opens NCAA Tournament without Caleb Wilson vs. surging VCU No. 6 seed North Carolina begins its NCAA Tournament run Thursday against No. 11 seed VCU while adjusting to life without star freshman Caleb Wilson. The Tar Heels have gone 5-3 since Wilson’s season-ending thumb injury. Posted 3/17/2026, 12:51:55 AM Updated 3/17/2026, 4:37:53 PM Related UNC sports UNC basketball Hubert Davis college basketball NCAA Tournament March Madness Selection Sunday Hubert Davis on VCU: We'll be ready for the challenge By Mark Bergin, WRAL senior multiplatform producer North Carolina will have somewhat of a homecourt advantage in the opening weekend of the 2026 NCAA Tournament if the Tar Heels can manage without star freshman Caleb Wilson. No. 6 seed UNC (24-8) plays No. 11 seed VCU (27-7) at 6:50 p.m. ET Thursday at Bon Secours Wellness Arena in Greenville, South Carolina. The South Region game airs on TNT. Other WRAL Top Stories Wilson, an All-ACC first-team selection, is out for the season after breaking his right thumb. Wilson injured the thumb while working back from a left hand fracture. Wilson's last game was on Feb. 20. UNC is 5-3 in eight games without Wilson. "Our team will move forward," UNC coach Hubert Davis said of Wilson's injury on March 7 after the regular-season finale against Duke. Davis added, "Obviously, there's tremendous sadness for him. I've talked about how special ... obviously he's a special player, but just how remarkable of a kid and a teammate he is and the passion that he has for his teammates, for North Carolina for being on the floor, for playing in games like this. It was a dream for him to play in the ACC [Tournament] and the NCAA Tournament, and my heart is broken that he won't be able to do that." The UNC-VCU winner will play the winner of the Illinois-Penn game on Saturday in Greenville, South Carolina. The other teams in the South Region include No. 1 seed Florida, No. 2 seed Houston, No. 4 seed Nebraska, No. 5 seed Vanderbilt, No. 7 seed Saint Mary’s, No. 8 seed Clemson, No. 9 seed Iowa, No. 10 seed Texas A&M, No. 12 seed McNeese, No. 13 seed Troy, No. 15 seed, Idaho, No. 16 seed Lehigh and No. 16 seed Prairie View A&M. UNC enters the NCAA Tournament on a two-game losing streak. Thursday marks the Tar Heels’ 55th appearance in the NCAA Tournament, the second most all-time. It is the fourth NCAA Tournament appearance in five seasons under head coach Hubert Davis, who has led the Tar Heels to an 8-3 record in the NCAA Tournament. The Tar Heels fell to Duke in the regular-season finale and lost 80-79 to Clemson in the ACC quarterfinals . Meanwhile, the Rams have won 16 of their last 17 games. It includes a three-game sweep of Duquesne, Saint Joseph's and Dayton to win the A-10 title this past weekend in Pittsburgh. result.text: UNC vs. VCU: What to know about the Rams' key players Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports Three VCU players UNC should be worried about North Carolina draws a dangerous No. 11 seed VCU in the NCAA Tournament. Here’s what to know about the Rams’ key players. Grant Chachere Tar Heels Wire North Carolina opens NCAA Tournament play in the South Region on Thursday, and unlike past years when it came in as a No. 6 seed or lower, the Tar Heels draw a dangerous first-round matchup with No. 11 seed VCU . The Rams have reached the NCAA Tournament 15 times since 2004. In those 15 appearances, they have advanced out of the first round five times, a stretch that includes a win over No. 6 seed Duke in 2007 and their memorable Cinderella run to the Final Four in 2011. With an expectation of not just making the field but winning games in March, VCU represents a far tougher opening test than many of the Tar Heels’ recent first-round opponents. VCU enters the tournament at 27-7 overall and went 15-3 in Atlantic 10 play, sharing the regular-season title with Dayton. The Rams captured the A-10 tournament championship with wins over Duquesne in the quarterfinals, Saint Joseph’s in the semifinals and Dayton in the title game. The Rams are 2-5 against Quad 1 opponents, with victories over South Florida and a road win at Dayton, and losses to NC State, Vanderbilt, Utah State and Saint Louis twice. Here are three players from the Rams to keep an eye on. Guard Terrence Hill (6-3, 180) Hill, one of two Rams named first-team All-Atlantic 10, is a combo guard who can score at all three levels and is an effective slasher, adept at creating drive-and-kick opportunities. Despite coming off the bench, Hill leads VCU in scoring at 14.4 points per game and in assists at 2.8 per game, earning Atlantic 10 sixth man of the year honors. Forward/Center Lazar Djokovic (6-11, 235) Djokovic is the Rams’ other first-team All-Atlantic 10 pick and a crucial presence in the frontcourt. At 6-foot-11, he is an athletic, skilled big who thrives in pick-and-roll action and can stretch defenses with his shooting. Djokovic is hitting 35.6% from 3-point range and is averaging 13.5 points and 5.4 rebounds while shooting 51.4% from the field. Guard Nyk Lewis (6-1, 195) Lewis was named the Atlantic 10 Freshman of the Year after averaging 8.7 points and 4.5 rebounds. A former top-100 recruit, he chose VCU over several power-conference programs. He also was a high school teammate of North Carolina point guard Derek Dixon at Gonzaga College High School in Washington, D.C.
